data.content:
质量保证体系
质量保证体系是企业建立并实施的体系，旨在确保药品按照批准的工艺规程和质量标准进行生产和控制。

质量控制
质量控制包括相应的组织机构、文件系统以及取样、检验等活动，旨在确保物料或产品在放行前完成必要的检验，确认其质量符合要求。

企业
企业是需要建立并实施质量保证体系、配备资源、建立各种管理程序并定期自检的组织。

高层管理人员
高层管理人员是企业的领导层，负责确保实现质量目标所必需的资源。

质量管理部门
质量管理部门是企业内负责参与所有与质量有关活动的部门。

偏差处理程序
偏差处理程序是企业建立的程序，用于记录、说明任何偏差，并对重大偏差进行调查，调查及结论需形成文件。

变更控制系统
变更控制系统是企业建立的系统，用于对可能影响产品质量的变更进行评估、批准和管理，并在实施后进行评价。

CAPA系统
CAPA系统(纠正和预防措施系统)是企业建立的系统，其纠正措施用于消除已发现的不符合项的原因，预防措施用于消除潜在不符合项的原因。

data.entity_name: 供应商评估和批准程序
data.content:
供应商评估和批准程序
供应商评估和批准程序是企业建立的程序，旨在确保原材料从认可的供应商处采购。

自检
自检是企业定期进行的活动，用于监控GMP的实施情况，并提出必要的纠正和预防措施。

药品
药品是按照批准的工艺规程和质量标准进行生产和控制的产品。

生产工艺
生产工艺是制定并经过验证的生产过程，旨在可持续稳定地生产出符合要求的产品。

中文说明书
中文说明书是清晰准确的产品使用说明。

操作人员
操作人员是经过培训的生产操作人员。

记录
记录是生产全过程的手工或仪器记录，用于追溯批产品的完整生产历史。

批产品
批产品是能够追溯完整生产历史的产品批次。

投诉
投诉是针对上市产品提出的，企业需要审查其原因并采取措施。

原材料
原材料是从认可供应商处采购的生产物料。

中间产品
中间产品是生产过程中的中间物料。

成品
成品是完成生产的最终产品。

中国GMP(2010年修订版)
中国GMP(2010年修订版)是中国药品生产质量管理规范的法规文件，规定了企业质量管理体系的基本要求。

召回系统
召回系统是企业建立的系统，能够召回任何一批已发运的产品，以降低质量风险。

质量目标
质量目标是企业需要实现的目标，高层管理人员应当确保实现质量目标所必需的资源。

质量标准
质量标准是药品生产和控制必须遵循的标准，确保药品质量符合要求。

工艺规程
工艺规程是经批准的药品生产工艺文件，药品应按照批准的工艺规程进行生产和控制。

偏差
偏差是生产过程中出现的偏离规定程序的情况，任何偏差都应当记录并说明，重大偏差应当进行调查。

变更
变更是可能影响产品质量的改变，实施前应当经过评估、批准，实施后应当进行评价。

验证
验证是对生产工艺及其重大变更进行确认的活动，确保其可持续稳定地生产出符合要求的产品。

培训
培训是操作人员必须接受的教育活动，确保其具备正确执行生产操作的能力。

资源
资源是实现质量目标所必需的人员、厂房、设施和设备等，由高层管理人员确保配备。

GMP
GMP(Good Manufacturing Practice)是药品生产质量管理规范，是企业实施质量管理的基本框架。<SEP>Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) is a set of requirements that must be contained within the Pharmaceutical Quality System.

质量风险
质量风险是产品在发运和使用过程中可能面临的质量隐患，企业应当最大限度地降低质量风险。

不符合项
不符合项是质量管理体系中发现的问题，包括已发现的不符合项和潜在的不符合项，CAPA系统用于消除其原因。

组织机构
质量控制包括相应的组织机构。

管理职责
质量保证体系确保管理职责被明确规定。

文件系统
质量控制包括相应的文件系统。

取样检验
质量控制包括取样、检验等活动。

人员
企业应当配备足够的具有相应资质的人员。

物料
质量控制确保物料在放行前完成必要的检验。

质量活动
质量管理部门应当参与所有与质量有关的活动。

厂房设施设备
企业应当配备足够的厂房、设施和设备。

生产步骤
质量保证体系确保按照要求完成了生产过程中各步骤。

产品
质量控制确保产品在放行前完成必要的检验。

调查
偏差均经过调查。

重大偏差
偏差处理程序对重大偏差进行调查。

产品发运
产品的发运能够最大限度地降低质量风险。

评估批准
变更实施前应当经过评估、批准。

评价
变更实施后应当进行评价，确认变更达到了预期目标。

GMP实施
自检用于监控GMP的实施情况。

纠正预防措施
自检提出必要的纠正和预防措施。

已发现不符合项
CAPA系统的纠正措施应当消除已发现的不符合项的原因。

潜在不符合项
CAPA系统的预防措施应当消除潜在不符合项的原因。

China GMP (2010 Revision) Chapter 8
Chapter 8 of the China GMP (2010 Revision) details the requirements for document management in pharmaceutical manufacturing.

Document Management System
A system that enterprises must establish to manage the drafting, revision, review, approval, distribution, replacement, and withdrawal of documents.

Document
A fundamental element of the quality assurance system, covering quality standards, production formulas, process procedures, operating procedures, and records.

Batch Production Record
A record created based on the approved current production formula and process procedure, designed to avoid transcription errors, and containing specific production details.

Batch Inspection Record
A record that must accompany each batch of drug, which must be true and complete, reflecting the entire production process.

Production Department
The department responsible for filling out batch production records.<SEP>The Production Department is an organizational unit from which the Quality Control Department must maintain independence.

Quality Control Department
The department responsible for reviewing batch records.<SEP>The Quality Control Department is an organizational unit that must be independent of the Production Department, responsible for setting internal standards, sampling, testing, stability evaluation, clean room monitoring, and laboratory management.

Data
Information that must be accurate and complete; changes to data must be justified, with the original data kept readable and the changer and date recorded.

Electronic Record
A type of record that must meet specific requirements, including access control, change logging, regular backups, and authorization for data entry or modification.

Drug
A pharmaceutical product for which batch production and inspection records must be maintained.

Authorized Personnel
Individuals who are permitted to enter or modify data in electronic records.

Shelf Life
The period during which a drug is considered usable; batch records must be kept for at least one year after its expiration, or for at least five years if no shelf life is specified.

Quality Assurance System
The overarching system for ensuring pharmaceutical quality, for which documents are described as a fundamental element.

Production Formula
The approved formula for drug production, which serves as the basis for creating batch production records.

Process Procedure
The approved manufacturing process steps, which serve as the basis for creating batch production records.

Key Process Parameters
Critical manufacturing parameters whose actual values must be recorded in the batch production record.

Material Balance Calculation
A calculation that must be included in the batch production record to account for material usage and yield.

Deviation Investigation Record
A record of deviations from standard procedures and their investigation, which must be included in the batch production record.

China GMP (2010 Revision)
China GMP (2010 Revision) is a national regulatory framework for pharmaceutical Good Manufacturing Practice in China, with Chapter 10 specifically addressing quality control and quality assurance requirements.

Quality Control
Quality Control refers to the systematic activities including sampling, testing, stability evaluation, and monitoring of materials, intermediates, and finished products to ensure they meet established standards.

Quality Assurance
Quality Assurance is a broad concept encompassing all planned and systematic activities implemented within the quality system to ensure that products meet quality requirements, as covered in Chapter 10 of China GMP.

Deviation Handling Procedure
The Deviation Handling Procedure is a formal process that includes deviation reporting and recording, investigation and evaluation, classification, corrective and preventive action development and implementation, and deviation closure.

Change Control Procedure
The Change Control Procedure is a formal process that includes change application and approval, classification and evaluation, implementation and verification, and change closure.

CAPA Procedure
The Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA) Procedure is a formal process that includes non-conformance reporting and recording, root cause analysis, corrective action development and implementation, preventive action development and implementation, and CAPA effectiveness verification.

Complaint Handling Procedure
The Complaint Handling Procedure is a formal process that includes complaint receipt and recording, investigation and evaluation, handling and response, and complaint trend analysis.

Recall Procedure
The Recall Procedure is a formal process that includes recall initiation and evaluation, implementation and monitoring, and closure and summary.

Self-Inspection Procedure
The Self-Inspection Procedure is a formal process that includes self-inspection plan development, implementation, report preparation, and corrective and preventive action follow-up.

Clean Room
A Clean Room (or clean area) is a controlled environment whose microbial and particulate levels must be monitored by the Quality Control Department.

Quality Control Laboratory
The Quality Control Laboratory is the facility where testing and analysis are performed, requiring its own set of management procedures.

Internal Control Standards
Internal Control Standards are specifications established and revised by the Quality Control Department for raw materials, intermediates, and finished products to ensure product quality.

Sampling Procedures
Sampling Procedures are operational guidelines developed by the Quality Control Department for the collection of representative samples from materials, intermediates, and finished products.

Testing Equipment
Testing Equipment refers to instruments, reagents, reference standards (or reference substances), standard solutions, and culture media used in quality control testing, which require management procedures.

Raw Materials
Raw Materials are input substances used in pharmaceutical manufacturing that are subject to sampling, testing, retention, and quality stability evaluation by the Quality Control Department.

Intermediates
Intermediates are in-process products during pharmaceutical manufacturing that are subject to sampling, testing, retention, and quality stability evaluation by the Quality Control Department.

Finished Products
Finished Products are the final pharmaceutical products that are subject to sampling, testing, retention, quality stability evaluation, and testing report issuance by the Quality Control Department.

Testing Reports
Testing Reports are formal documents issued by the Quality Control Department after sampling, testing, and retention of raw materials, intermediates, and finished products.

Deviation Investigation
Deviation Investigation is a systematic process that includes deviation description, root cause analysis, impact assessment on product quality, corrective measures, and preventive measures.

Root Cause Analysis
Root Cause Analysis is a systematic method used in deviation investigation and CAPA procedures to identify the fundamental cause of a non-conformance or deviation.

Corrective And Preventive Actions
Corrective And Preventive Actions (CAPA) are measures developed and implemented to address identified non-conformances, deviations, and to prevent their recurrence, with effectiveness verification required.

ICH Q10
ICH Q10 is a guideline that describes the Pharmaceutical Quality System (PQS) for pharmaceutical products, based on ICH Q8, ICH Q9, and itself.

ICH Q8
ICH Q8 is a guideline focused on pharmaceutical development, serving as one of the foundational references for ICH Q10.

ICH Q9
ICH Q9 is a guideline focused on quality risk management, serving as one of the foundational references for ICH Q10.<SEP>ICH Q9 is a pharmaceutical guideline that defines the systematic process of quality risk management for assessing, controlling, communicating, and reviewing quality risks throughout the product lifecycle.

Pharmaceutical Quality System
The Pharmaceutical Quality System (PQS) is a comprehensive framework that encompasses GMP requirements, quality risk management, knowledge management, and continuous improvement for ensuring pharmaceutical product quality.

Quality Risk Management
Quality Risk Management is a systematic approach to assessing, controlling, communicating, and reviewing risks to the quality of pharmaceutical products across the product lifecycle.<SEP>Quality Risk Management (QRM) is a systematic process for assessing, controlling, communicating, and reviewing quality risks of pharmaceutical products throughout the product lifecycle.

Knowledge Management
Knowledge Management is a systematic method for collecting, analyzing, updating, and transferring data and knowledge to improve pharmaceutical processes and products.

data.entity_name: Process Performance and Product Quality Monitoring
data.content:
Process Performance and Product Quality Monitoring
Process Performance and Product Quality Monitoring is a system for establishing monitoring of process performance and product quality, conducting periodic process performance evaluations, and implementing product quality reviews.

CAPA
Corrective Actions and Preventive Actions (CAPA) is a system that uses investigation methods to determine root causes of deviations, takes measures to prevent recurrence, validates effectiveness, and integrates with the knowledge management system.

Change Management
Change Management is a system that evaluates the impact of changes on product quality, requires changes to be approved, ensures planned and documented implementation, and confirms that changes achieve expected objectives through post-change review.<SEP>Change management is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to evaluate and control risks associated with changes in pharmaceutical processes or systems.

Continuous Improvement
Continuous Improvement is a culture and approach that uses data-driven methods and periodically evaluates the effectiveness of improvement measures within the pharmaceutical quality system.

Senior Management
Senior Management holds ultimate responsibility for the Pharmaceutical Quality System, including establishing clear quality objectives, ensuring resource availability, establishing organizational structure, and ensuring employee understanding and implementation.

Periodic Review of PQS
Periodic Review of PQS is a scheduled evaluation of the effectiveness of the Pharmaceutical Quality System, covering process performance, product quality, and CAPA, with results used for continuous improvement.

Quality Objectives
Quality Objectives are clear targets established by Senior Management as part of their responsibility for the Pharmaceutical Quality System.

Root Cause Investigation
Root Cause Investigation is a systematic method used by the CAPA system to determine the underlying causes of deviations and quality issues.

Product Quality
Product Quality refers to the overall quality characteristics of pharmaceutical products that must be monitored, maintained, and improved through the Pharmaceutical Quality System.

Product Quality Review
Product Quality Review is a periodic evaluation implemented as part of the Process Performance and Product Quality Monitoring system to assess the quality of pharmaceutical products.

Process Performance Evaluation
Process Performance Evaluation is a periodic assessment conducted to monitor and evaluate the performance of pharmaceutical manufacturing processes.

Risk Assessment
Risk assessment is a component of the quality risk management process that includes risk identification, risk analysis, and risk evaluation.

Risk Control
Risk control is a component of the quality risk management process that includes risk reduction and risk acceptance.

Risk Review
Risk review is a component of the quality risk management process that involves periodically reviewing risk management decisions.

Risk Identification
Risk identification is the first step of risk assessment, focused on identifying potential hazards and risks associated with those hazards.

Risk Analysis
Risk analysis is the second step of risk assessment, involving estimation of identified risks through evaluation of their severity and probability of occurrence.

Risk Evaluation
Risk evaluation is the third step of risk assessment, involving comparison of identified and analyzed risks against given risk criteria.

Risk Reduction
Risk reduction is a component of risk control that involves taking measures to reduce risk when it exceeds acceptable levels.

Risk Acceptance
Risk acceptance is a component of risk control that involves deciding to accept the level of residual risk.

FMEA
Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) is a commonly used quality risk management tool for evaluating potential failure modes and their effects.

FTA
Fault Tree Analysis (FTA) is a commonly used quality risk management tool for identifying root causes of failures through logical tree diagrams.

HACCP
Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) is a commonly used quality risk management tool for identifying hazards and establishing critical control points.

HAZOP
Hazard and Operability Analysis (HAZOP) is a commonly used quality risk management tool for identifying hazards and operability problems.

PHA
Preliminary Hazard Analysis (PHA) is a commonly used quality risk management tool for identifying potential hazards at an early stage.

Risk Ranking And Filtering
Risk ranking and filtering is a commonly used quality risk management tool for prioritizing risks based on defined criteria.

Production Process Control
Production process control is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to manage risks in pharmaceutical manufacturing processes.

Deviation Handling
Deviation handling is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to assess and manage risks arising from deviations in pharmaceutical processes.

Supplier Management
Supplier management is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to evaluate and control risks related to suppliers of materials and services.

Facility And Equipment Design
Facility and equipment design is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to identify and mitigate risks in the design of pharmaceutical facilities and equipment.

Validation And Confirmation
Validation and confirmation is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to ensure that processes and systems meet predetermined specifications and quality requirements.

Document Management
Document management is an application area where quality risk management can be applied to manage risks associated with pharmaceutical documentation and record-keeping.
